comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request significantly enhances the API's ability to interact with Temporal workflows by introducing direct querying capabilities. It ensures that execution lists and details retrieved from the '/api/executions' endpoints can be authoritatively sourced from Temporal, improving data accuracy and consistency. The changes include robust handling of workflow IDs, advanced filtering options, and comprehensive documentation of the feature's design and implementation, all driven by an automated specification and development process. ### Highlights * **Direct Temporal Querying**: Implemented direct querying of Temporal for execution lists and details via a new 'source=temporal' query parameter in the '/api/executions' endpoints, ensuring authoritative data retrieval. * **Enhanced Execution State Mapping**: Improved the mapping of Temporal workflow states to internal projection models, specifically for 'mm_state' and 'waiting_reason', to reflect accurate workflow status. * **Authoritative Read Integration**: Integrated authoritative Temporal reads into task detail and listing services to ensure data consistency when 'temporal_authoritative_read_enabled' is active. * **Workflow ID Canonicalization**: Added logic to correctly handle and canonicalize workflow IDs with the 'mm:' prefix for accurate Temporal lookups across API endpoints. * **Comprehensive Specification & Testing**: Introduced detailed specification documents covering the feature's requirements, plan, data model, and testing strategy, along with new unit tests for the Temporal API behaviors. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Changelog * **api_service/api/routers/executions.py** * Added 'source' query parameter to 'list_executions' and 'describe_execution' functions. * Implemented logic to directly query Temporal for execution lists when 'source="temporal"' is specified. * Updated 'describe_execution' to perform authoritative reads from Temporal when 'source="temporal"' or 'temporal_authoritative_read_enabled' is true. * Improved error handling for Temporal service unavailability, returning a 503 status code. * Removed a redundant 'global _shared_client_adapter' declaration. * **api_service/core/sync.py** * Modified 'map_temporal_state_to_projection' to extract 'mm_state' from search attributes when a workflow is running. * Enhanced 'map_temporal_state_to_projection' to infer 'waiting_reason' as 'external_completion' if the state is 'AWAITING_EXTERNAL' and no reason is explicitly set. * Updated the 'awaiting_external' field in the projection to directly reflect the 'MoonMindWorkflowState.AWAITING_EXTERNAL' value. * **moonmind/workflows/tasks/compatibility.py** * Added logic to 'get_task_detail' to sync execution projections from Temporal if 'temporal_authoritative_read_enabled' is true. * Integrated authoritative Temporal reads into '_load_temporal_rows' to sync multiple execution projections from Temporal if 'temporal_authoritative_read_enabled' is true and records exist. * **moonmind/workflows/tasks/source_mapping.py** * Updated 'resolve_task' to canonicalize the 'task_id' using 'TemporalExecutionRecord.canonicalize_identifier' before fetching mappings or probing backing records. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/checklists/requirements.md** * Added a new specification quality checklist for Temporal API Consistency. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/contracts/requirements-traceability.md** * Added a new requirements traceability matrix for the Temporal API Consistency feature. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/data-model.md** * Added a new data model document outlining enhanced execution model fields for Temporal data. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/plan.md** * Added a new implementation plan for Temporal API Consistency, including technical context, constitution checks, and project structure. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/quickstart.md** * Added a new quickstart guide for validating Temporal API consistency changes locally. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/research.md** * Added a new research document detailing design decisions on authoritative reads, 'mm:' prefixes, and filtering with search attributes. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/spec.md** * Added a new feature specification for Temporal API Consistency, including user stories, functional requirements, and success criteria. * **specs/task/20260308/b8f26474-multi/tasks.md** * Added a new task breakdown document for the Temporal API Consistency feature, detailing phases and dependencies. * **step-11-report.md** * Added a new report detailing findings from a specification analysis, noting hallucinated requirements and tasks. * **step-12-report.md** * Added a new report on remediation application, confirming traceability mappings for functional requirements. * **step-13-report.md** * Added a new report summarizing specification analysis, requirement coverage, and alignment with the constitution. * **step-16-report.md** * Updated the final report to reflect the current feature path, edited files, test status, and scope validation for Temporal API consistency. * **step-3-report.md** * Added a new report confirming the 'Safe-to-Implement' determination for the feature. * **tests/unit/api/test_executions_temporal.py** * Added a new unit test file for Temporal-specific API endpoint behaviors. * Included tests for listing executions with 'source=temporal', bypassing the database. * Added tests for describing executions with 'source=temporal' and verifying projection syncing. * Included tests for canonicalization of 'mm:' prefixed workflow IDs. * Added tests to ensure a 503 status code is returned on Temporal service unavailability.
Activity * The pull request was automatically generated by the MoonMind queue publish stage. * A series of specification and planning documents were created and refined, including a quality checklist, requirements traceability, data model, implementation plan, quickstart guide, research decisions, and detailed feature specification. * Reports were generated at various steps (step-3, step-11, step-12, step-13, step-16) to track the progress, analyze the specification, apply remediations, and confirm the 'Safe-to-Implement' status. * Unit tests for the new Temporal API behaviors were added and passed successfully, validating the implementation. * The final report (step-16) confirmed 100% DOC-REQ coverage and readiness for handoff, indicating the feature is fully scoped and consistent.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for Github and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG). You can also get AI-powered code generation, chat, as well as code reviews directly in the IDE at no cost with the [Gemini Code Assist IDE Extension](https://cloud.google.com/products/gemini/code-assist).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a direct query path to Temporal for listing and describing executions, controlled by a `source=temporal` query parameter, a key step towards making Temporal the authoritative source of truth. However, the `list_executions` endpoint is vulnerable to query injection due to unsanitized user-supplied parameters being concatenated into the Temporal Visibility query string, which could allow an authenticated attacker to access unauthorized workflow metadata. The `describe_execution` endpoint appears to be correctly protected. Additionally, there are areas for improvement regarding code duplication, exception handling, and the management of the shared Temporal client instance.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The use of `global _shared_client_adapter` and its repeated, non-thread-safe initialization in multiple places (`list_executions`, `describe_execution`, and `moonmind/workflows/tasks/compatibility.py`) can lead to maintainability and concurrency issues. A better approach is to manage the `TemporalClientAdapter` lifecycle using FastAPI's dependency injection system. This would centralize client creation, ensure it's a singleton for the application's lifetime, and make the code cleaner and more testable.

Consider creating a dependency function to provide the adapter, which can then be injected into your route handlers.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

This block of code, which handles fetching and syncing an execution from Temporal, is nearly identical to the logic in `api_service/api/routers/executions.py` (in `describe_execution`). There is similar duplication for list operations in `_load_temporal_rows` (lines 397-432).

This duplication makes the code harder to maintain, as any changes to the sync logic will need to be applied in multiple places. This functionality should be extracted into a shared utility function, perhaps in `api_service/core/sync.py`, that can be called from both `compatibility.py` and `executions.py`.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Catching a broad `Exception` can mask underlying programming errors (e.g., `TypeError`, `AttributeError`) as a "Temporal service unavailable" error, making debugging more difficult. It's better to catch more specific exceptions related to network or RPC failures from the `temporalio` library, such as `temporalio.service.RPCError`. This allows other unexpected exceptions to fail loudly, which is often preferable for robustness.

```suggestion
        except RPCError as exc:
            logger.warning("Failed to list Temporal executions directly: %s", exc, exc_info=True)
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_503_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,
                detail={"code": "temporal_unavailable", "message": "Temporal service unavailable."},
            ) from exc
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Similar to the other `try...except` block, catching a broad `Exception` here can hide the true cause of an error. It's better to catch specific exceptions from the `temporalio` library (e.g., `temporalio.service.RPCError`) to distinguish between Temporal service unavailability and other application-level bugs.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `except ValueError: pass` block silently ignores cases where `mm_state` from Temporal's search attributes has an invalid value. This can make it difficult to debug issues with workflows that are reporting an incorrect state. Consider adding a log warning to make these situations visible.

```python
            except ValueError:
                logger.warning("Invalid value for mm_state search attribute: '%s'", mm_state)
```

## Module is imported with 'import' and 'import from' Module 'api_service.api.routers.executions' is imported with both 'import' and 'import from'. --- In general, to fix "Module is imported with 'import' and 'import from'" you should avoid importing the same module using both syntaxes. Either (1) use only import module and reference its attributes through the module, or (2) use only from module import name and, if you need the module object itself, obtain it without a second import (e.g., via sys.modules).

Here, the tests already use from api_service.api.routers.executions import _get_service, router at the top. The only reason for the later import api_service.api.routers.executions as exec_module statements is to assign to exec_module._shared_client_adapter. We can keep the existing top-level import unchanged and, inside each test, replace the import ... as exec_module with a lookup from sys.modules:

import sys
exec_module = sys.modules["api_service.api.routers.executions"]

This preserves the behavior (we still get the module object and can set _shared_client_adapter = None) while removing the second, conflicting import form. Concretely:

  • Add an import sys at the top of tests/unit/api/test_executions_temporal.py alongside the other imports.
  • In test_list_executions_source_temporal_bypasses_db_and_queries_temporal, replace the import api_service.api.routers.executions as exec_module line with getting the module from sys.modules.
  • Do the same replacement in test_describe_execution_source_temporal_syncs_projection and test_temporal_unavailability_returns_503.

No other functionality changes are needed.

## Module is imported with 'import' and 'import from' Module 'api_service.api.routers.executions' is imported with both 'import' and 'import from'. --- In general, to fix “module imported with both import and from”, you consolidate imports so the module is imported in only one consistent way. Either (a) only from module import name1, name2 and never import module, or (b) import the module object once and access its attributes via that object, but don’t also use from ... import ... for the same module.

For this file, the least invasive and clearest fix is:

  • Add a single module-level import for the executions router module, e.g. import api_service.api.routers.executions as executions_module, near the other imports.
  • Remove the three in-function imports:
    • line 49 in test_list_executions_source_temporal_bypasses_db_and_queries_temporal
    • line 103 in test_describe_execution_source_temporal_syncs_projection
    • line 199 in test_temporal_unavailability_returns_503
  • Replace uses of exec_module._shared_client_adapter with executions_module._shared_client_adapter (or the chosen alias) in those three tests.
  • Keep the existing from api_service.api.routers.executions import _get_service, router as-is; the rule is satisfied because the “bare module” is now only imported once, and the “from” import is also only done once at the top.

    • All required pieces are standard imports; no new methods or external libraries are needed, just the additional top-level module import and the replacement of the local imports and references.

## Module is imported with 'import' and 'import from' Module 'api_service.api.routers.executions' is imported with both 'import' and 'import from'. --- In general, to fix “Module is imported with both import and from” issues, you should pick one import style for that module within the file and use it consistently. Here, we already have a from api_service.api.routers.executions import _get_service, router at the top. The tests that need to access the module object just to set _shared_client_adapter currently do a second import with import api_service.api.routers.executions as exec_module. The cleanest fix is to import the module once at the top (using a standard import ... as ...) and then reuse that binding everywhere, instead of re-importing it inside each test.

Concretely, in tests/unit/api/test_executions_temporal.py:

  • Add a top-level import of the module object, e.g. import api_service.api.routers.executions as executions_module, next to the existing from api_service.api.routers.executions import _get_service, router.
  • Replace each inner import api_service.api.routers.executions as exec_module with assignments that use the top-level module, e.g. executions_module._shared_client_adapter = None.
  • Remove any use of the now-undefined exec_module name; only executions_module will be used.

    • This keeps functionality identical: _shared_client_adapter is still reset before each test, but we no longer import the same module using two different syntaxes within the same file.

## Empty except 'except' clause does nothing but pass and there is no explanatory comment. --- In general, empty except blocks should either be removed (if they are not needed), narrowed and handled properly (e.g., with logging, fallback behavior, or re-raising), or documented with a clear comment explaining why the exception is intentionally ignored. Here, we do want to keep the functional behavior: if mm_state cannot be mapped to MoonMindWorkflowState, we should leave state_value unchanged. The best fix is to add a small body to the except ValueError: that records the problem via the existing logger, without changing control flow or state_value.

Concretely, in api_service/core/sync.py, within map_temporal_state_to_projection, at lines 111–114:

111:             try:
112:                 state_value = MoonMindWorkflowState(str(mm_state))
113:             except ValueError:
114:                 pass

we should replace the pass with a logger.warning (or similar) explaining that the mm_state value is invalid and will be ignored. This uses the already-defined logger at the top of the file and introduces no new imports or dependencies. The rest of the function remains unchanged, so existing functionality (falling back to the previous state_value) is preserved, while satisfying the CodeQL rule and improving observability.

## Module is imported with 'import' and 'import from' Module 'api_service.api.routers.executions' is imported with both 'import' and 'import from'. --- In general, to fix “Module is imported with 'import' and 'import from'” you should choose one import style for that module within the file and use it consistently. Either (1) keep from module import name1, name2 and remove the plain import module as alias, updating use sites, or (2) keep import module as alias and refer to all members through that alias.

The least invasive fix here is to keep the existing import api_service.api.routers.executions as executions_module (since it’s used several times) and remove the from api_service.api.routers.executions import _get_service, router line. Then, update the few usages of _get_service and router to use the alias: executions_module._get_service and executions_module.router. This preserves functionality, keeps type checking behavior the same, and satisfies the CodeQL rule.

Concretely, in tests/unit/api/test_executions_temporal.py:

  • Remove the from api_service.api.routers.executions import _get_service, router line.
  • Change app.include_router(router) to app.include_router(executions_module.router).
  • Change app.dependency_overrides[_get_service] = ... to app.dependency_overrides[executions_module._get_service] = ....

No new methods or complex definitions are needed beyond these reference updates; we only rely on the already imported executions_module.
